answer: The `order-service` container will start only when the `rabbitmq` service is healthy. If the `rabbitmq` service is not healthy, the `order-service` container will not start.
Four distinct health checks are defined. Their common parameters are: test, interval, timeout, and retries.
The default infrastructure provider is Terraform, and the alternative provider is Bicep. This configuration is specified in the infra section of the azure.yaml file.
The `store-front` container would fail to start if the `product-service` and `order-service` containers are not running, as it depends on both. These dependencies must be satisfied first.

Clone the sample application source from GitHub using git.  
Create a directory (e.g., demorepo), switch to it, and run `git clone https://github.com/Azure-Samples/aks-store-demo.git`.  
Change into the cloned directory with `cd aks-store-demo`.  
If using azd, create an empty directory named aks-store-demo, change into it, and run `azd init --template aks-store-demo` to clone the azd template.  
Review the docker-compose-quickstart YAML file from the cloned repository.  
Use Docker Compose to build container images and start the application with `docker compose -f docker-compose-quickstart.yml up -d`.  
View created images using `docker images`.  
View running containers using `docker ps`.  
Navigate to http://localhost:8080 in a web browser to validate the running application.  
Tools used: git, azd (if using azd template), Docker Compose, web browser.
